summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app to a VS Code extension to reduce install friction (auto-detect .entire/settings.json instead of manual repo registration) and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view, native checkpoint tree sidebar, MCP server embedded at /mcp, search via QuickPick with FTS, image rendering, agent-aware labels and CLI commands, VSIX packaging script, and onboarding flow for new users.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite DB at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db; global config at ~/.gossamer/config.json is auto-created by writeConfig() on first repo registration

Custom session titles from /rename are stored in 'custom-title' events in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/*.jsonl, NOT in shadow or checkpoint branches — auto-generated slugs come from LogEvent.slug
The .gossamer/ directory inside each repo is an intentional git worktree for the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch used by the indexer; safe to add to .gitignore

better-sqlite3 native module version mismatch between VS Code's Electron Node.js and system Node.js requires spawning the gossamer server as a child process using system node (via 'which node'), not process.execPath
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps checkpointId to gitOid; session cwd must be resolved from CheckpointSessionMetadata → ShadowSession to find the correct repo for git operations
Image data is stored as base64 in full.jsonl on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch; the LogContent schema needed imageData and imageMediaType fields added
The gossamer-testing worktree has its own source file copies that must be manually synced from the main repo and rebuilt independently every time

Prisma CLI is 60MB+ and cannot be bundled in a VSIX; the correct approach is generating a schema.sql via 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty' at build time and applying it with $executeRawUnsafe at startup
Agent name comes from CheckpointSessionMetadata.agent; CLI command mapping (AGENT_CLI) needed for correct resume/new-session commands per agent

code.finding: Original include field listed only 'src/extension.ts' and 'src/GossamerPanel.ts'; SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were never compiled, causing blank diff views. Fixed by changing to 'src/**/*.ts' with 'src/webview' excluded.
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview; store result as window.__vscodeApi and reuse in vscodeApi.ts singleton to avoid 'already called' error when extension host also calls it.

pushSchema() SQL parsing bug: schema.sql has '-- CreateTable' comment blocks before each statement; filtering with startsWith('--') discarded ALL statements. Correct fix: split on ';', then strip line-level comments from each chunk before executing.

Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null at []−dep useEffect time; checking scroll position inline at the moment the renderItems effect fires (el.scrollHeight − el.scrollTop − el.clientHeight < 80) is more reliable than a separate scroll listener.
resourceUri for SCM color decorations requires absolute paths; the tree provider must store repoPath and compute absPath = join(repoPath, fullPath) for each file/dir item.

Generated via 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema ... --script' and transformed with sed to use IF NOT EXISTS. Safe to re-apply against existing DBs for fresh install initialization without Prisma CLI at runtime.

workflow: VS Code webview CSP requires 'img-src data: https: http:' for base64 image rendering; default-src 'none' blocks all images silently
File watchers (fs.watch) with 30s fallback polls provide near-real-time updates more reliably than pure polling; debounce watcher events (50ms for DB, 200ms for git refs)
TextDocumentContentProvider with a custom URI scheme (gossamer-diff:, gossamer-summary:) is cleaner than temp files for read-only virtual documents in VS Code diff views
process.kill(pid, 0) pattern detects running processes: ESRCH = gone, EPERM = running as different user but alive, no error = running
Static server singleton pattern (GossamerPanel.server as static) lets the server survive panel disposal for minimize/restore behavior
onDidChangeViewState must be used to re-send server_ready when a webview panel is moved to a new window, as VS Code reloads the webview HTML from scratch
VS Code chord keybindings (Ctrl+K G) may not fire when a webview has keyboard focus; webview-side postMessage is more reliable for triggering extension-host actions from within the webview
When packaging a VS Code extension with native Node.js modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty), esbuild --external those modules and copy their full node_modules trees alongside the bundle

friction: gossamer-testing worktree requires manual file sync (cp) from main repo and independent rebuild on every change — error-prone and caused multiple 'still seeing old behavior' debugging loops
VS Code's Electron Node.js NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch with system Node.js (141 vs 140) was a hard blocker that required architectural change (spawn server as child process)
Accidentally applied changes to the Electron app multiple times despite the project being VS Code-only; user had to correct this explicitly
SQL schema parsing bug (comment filtering) caused fresh install failures that were hard to diagnose because the error appeared deep in startup and the server failed silently
Prisma CLI cannot be bundled (60MB+), requiring a separate schema.sql generation step and making schema migrations more complex
Server port coordination: random getFreePort() made MCP config impossible; required stable DEFAULT_PORT and config-file-based port resolution
Custom session titles require reading ~/.claude/projects/ JSONL files directly with mtime-based caching — not available in the checkpoint/shadow branches where everything else is
gossamer.vsix and bundled-server/ artifacts committed to git before user noticed and removed them
open_items: Schema migration for existing DBs: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS handles fresh installs but ALTER TABLE for new columns on existing DBs is not implemented — will require explicit migration statements when schema changes
gossamer-testing worktree sync is manual; a watch script or shared symlinks would prevent stale-build confusion
Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G) does not reliably fire when the webview has keyboard focus
Session color selection data is not persisted anywhere in Claude's files (in-memory UI state only) — cannot be displayed
Electron app (app/) remains in the repo but is unmaintained; should be removed or clearly marked deprecated
gossamer.vsix and packages/vscode/bundled-server/ should be added to .gitignore
If a session is renamed with no subsequent events, the custom title won't appear until the next hook event fires
The install script (scripts/install-mcp.mjs) covers Claude Code and Codex CLI but not other MCP-compatible agents
